title: salix caprea var. angustifolia
intro: Salix caprea var. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved goat willow or narrow-leaved sallow, is a variety of the goat willow plant, characterized by its narrow leaves and shrub-like growth habit. Traditionally, the bark of this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Salix caprea var. angustifolia is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
